behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is at a module handoff boundary (three source clients -> aggregation/payload builder -> summary step), not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA: No real OS/network/subprocess is required to catch this specific failure; in-process fakes for the three sources can still expose missing/overwritten sections. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking at the source-client boundary does not inherently hide the bug, because the assertion is about merged payload completeness. Rejected higher level: System is disqualified by behavior text focusing on 'content passed to the summary-generation step' (handoff correctness), not transport/network characteristics. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because this behavior does not evaluate model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real aggregation/orchestration modules with three stubbed source adapters returning distinct sentinel data; execute collection + payload assembly; assert the object/string sent to summary generation contains all three source sections and values.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This is cross-module error propagation and fallback rendering (source adapter failure -> report assembly), so Unit is too low. REAL-INFRA: The behavior can be reproduced with an in-process failing adapter (exception/timeout error object); real network outage is not required for the core fallback logic. MOCK-HIDE: A total mock of orchestration could hide issues, but module-level wiring with a failing adapter will expose them. Rejected higher level: System is disqualified because behavior text is about graceful degradation ('note ... rather than aborting'), not real socket/OS semantics. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because correctness does not depend on model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real collection/orchestration path with two successful source adapters and one adapter that fails; verify report generation completes, includes `data unavailable` for the failed section, and still includes other sections.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: This behavior is explicitly about summary accuracy/faithfulness, which depends on real model output quality. A deterministic stub would always pass and cannot reveal hallucination, omission, or misstatement. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking the model hides the exact failure class this behavior targets. MULTI-STEP: Only one agentic decision point is required (summary generation), so Workflow is not required. Rejected lower level: Integration is disqualified by behavior text 'accurately represents' because that is model-judgment quality, not just routing/retry/payload format.
behaviors.test_description: Execute summary generation against the real model using representative weekly datasets with known key values; assert factual consistency checks (key metrics/issues/deploy counts preserved, no contradictory claims) with tolerance rules over repeated runs.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: This requires real email transport/inbox behavior (SMTP/API delivery, recipient routing, message acceptance), which in-process mocks can falsely pass. MOCK-HIDE: Mock email clients hide real failures like auth, TLS, provider rejection, or inbox routing. LLM-DEP check: correctness criterion here is delivery to inbox, not summary quality; model-output quality is not the asserted boundary. Rejected higher level: Agentic is disqualified by behavior text 'reaches ... inbox correctly' because it tests transport end-to-end, not whether model decisions are good. Workflow is disqualified because no multi-agentic sequence is being validated.
behaviors.test_description: Run the scheduled script end-to-end in a test environment with reachable real/staging data endpoints and a real mail service + test inbox; verify message is received by configured recipient with expected subject/body structure and attachments/sections present.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: The failure mode spans real network unreachability plus filesystem persistence and next-run retry behavior, crossing OS/time/process boundaries. MOCK-HIDE: Pure mocks can miss path/permission/write durability and real retry-on-next-execution behavior. MULTI-STEP: This is temporal retry across runs but not multiple agentic decisions, so Workflow is unnecessary. Rejected higher level: Agentic is disqualified because behavior text is about delivery fallback/retry mechanics, not AI output quality.
behaviors.test_description: In a system test, force mail endpoint unreachability, execute scheduled run, verify report is written to local fallback file; then restore mail service and trigger next scheduled run, verifying queued report is retried and delivered.
